title: Metabolic profiling with HUMAnN
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c2c5m","text":"In this protocol, we perform a metabolic profiling of metagenomic datasets using HUMAnN applied to the 20 samples taxonomically profiled in the other protocols contained in this collection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":81,"length":6}],"data":[]},{"key":"d5n94","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"721tm","text":"Once the metabolic profile has been generated, the following protocols can be performed:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cpetg","text":"MetaPhlAn output merge and visualizations","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7bs6p","text":"GraPhlAn visualization of single and multiple samples","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"epsq9","text":"Taxonomic biomarker discovery with LEfSe","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"F6578D6638B3421599E433DAFC5D12E3","url":"http://huttenhower.sph.harvard.edu/humann"}}}}

HUMAnN can be obtained using Mercurial: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":14,"length":6},{"key":1,"offset":22,"length":5},{"key":2,"offset":79,"length":9}],"data":[]},{"key":"677fg","text":"hg clone ssh://hg@bitbucket.org/chuttenh/humann","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":0,"length":47}],"data":[]},{"key":"91t7n","text":" or using the direct links to the zip, gz, or bz2 archives.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"offset":34,"length":3},{"key":5,"offset":39,"length":2},{"key":6,"offset":46,"length":3}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4C1426FAD5274A3F869F0CE851C999D1","url":"http://huttenhower.sph.harvard.edu/humann"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"19CA743DEE5743209244F966B978AF2D","url":"http://www.scons.org/"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"654564CFC4394CE7B9AFAE46E4AAC5E3","url":"http://mercurial.selenic.com/"}},"3":{"type":"code_insert","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":"hg clone ssh:\/\/hg@bitbucket.org\/chuttenh\/humann"},"4":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"9D6FDB00BB3446FC9F28E88119A2DD68","url":"https://bitbucket.org/chuttenh/humann/get/default.zip"}},"5":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4188CA13292A4341B8302E0581D08F0F","url":"https://bitbucket.org/chuttenh/humann/get/default.gz"}},"6":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"CF8B51B1BCB745F2A735721C5E493A11","url":"https://bitbucket.org/chuttenh/humann/get/default.bz2"}}}}
steps: Perform a translated search (using blastx or usearch ) against the KEGG DB. Since HUMAnN's development KEGG has become commercial, we are currently developing support for other data sources.

Place the translated BLAST results using KEGG gene identifiers in the input input directory. Optionally can be gzipped or bzipped, several other formats can be enabled editing the settings in the SConstruct file.
Run the scons scons command, optionally parallelizing multiple analyses using the -j -j flag. Results will be placed in the "output" directory.
We provide the HUMAnN output for users who want to perform the downstream analysis pipeline but avoid the computational intensive steps above. The 20 samples profiled with HUMAnN are available here .

Using HUMAnN output, you can perform the metabolic counterpart of the taxonomic pipeline presented in this tutorial. We report below the first command to obtain the merged table of metabolic abundances. $ mkdir output$ utils/merge_metaphlan_tables.py humann_profiling/*.txt > output/merged_humann_abundance_table.txt $ mkdir output $ utils/merge_metaphlan_tables.py humann_profiling/*.txt > output/merged_humann_abundance_table.txt
creator: Bahar Sayoldin
